prose:
Frontier-orbital single-point calculation on aniline (14 atoms, charge 0, multiplicity 1) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', RI density fitting) in PySCF, gas phase. Geometry: user-supplied .xyz file at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz (not re-optimized at this level — single point only).

SCF converged in 9 cycles. No warnings reported (warnings array empty — clean run). Integrity status: trustworthy = true (all checks passed).

Headline result — HOMO–LUMO gap: 5.3048177 eV
HOMO (index 24): −5.553772 eV
LUMO (index 25): −0.2489544 eV

Total electronic energy: −287.71578 Hartree (−7829.1453 eV). This energy zero is method/basis-specific and not comparable across levels of theory.

Koopmans-derived descriptors (orbital approximation, not ΔE values):
  Vertical IP (−ε_HOMO): 5.553772 eV
  Vertical EA (−ε_LUMO): 0.2489544 eV
  Electronegativity χ: 2.9013632 eV
  Chemical hardness η: 2.6524088 eV
  Chemical softness S: 0.37701579 eV⁻¹
  Electrophilicity index ω: 1.5868422 eV

Method: DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF RKS, RI density fitting), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1.
